chunk_summary.idea_trajectory_justification: The group is actively brainstorming and expanding the research scope by identifying new cell types and signaling mechanisms to investigate.
chunk_summary.collective_engagement_justification: Participants maintain consistent eye contact with the camera and provide regular, audible backchannels and nods while others are speaking.
chunk_summary.cross_disciplinary_bridging_description: Connects immunology (cytokine/cytokine receptor signaling) with neurobiology (neurotransmitter signaling) to explore cross-talk.
chunk_summary.artifact_interaction_description: Participants reference the specific questions listed on the shared slide to guide their discussion.
chunk_summary.problem_specificity_justification: The group has moved from a general topic to specific questions about cell-specific receptor expression and signaling.
chunk_summary.decision_crystallization_justification: The group has identified a shared interest in the cross-talk between immune and neural cells but has not yet defined a specific project.
chunk_summary.complementarity_recognition_quote: the role of the complement is very important process in synaptic pruning
chunk_summary.skill_gap_description: Sarkis identifies a gap in knowledge regarding in vivo versus in vitro cytokine signaling and seeks input from others.
chunk_summary.shared_vision_quote: How can we understand the interchangeable role of neurons and immune cells?
chunk_summary.notable_observation: The group successfully transitioned from a broad, abstract topic to a more concrete, collaborative research question by using the shared slide as a focal point.

ideas_currently_on_table: Interchangeable roles of neurons and immune cells
Microglia depletion and cytokine receptor knockout
chunk_summaries: The group brainstormed the cross-talk between immune and neural cells, specifically focusing on cytokine and neurotransmitter signaling. Sarkis led the discussion by framing the research problem, while other participants contributed domain-specific knowledge about cell types and experimental strategies. The group ended the chunk in a divergent state, having identified several promising research avenues but no specific project.
